skill.name: recovery
skill.description: Recover session state from memory-keeper after context loss.
skill.summary: Recover session state from memory-keeper after context loss.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but AI review found these are false positives in Markdown examples and placeholders. SKILL.md contains recovery guidance for memory-keeper context and no executable shell, Ruby, cryptography, network, filesystem, credential, or prompt injection behavior. No evidence found of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Fenced Recovery Examples
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to Markdown fences and inline placeholder names, including context_get and context_summarize examples. They are documentation for expected tool calls, not executable Ruby backticks or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside Markdown code fences or checklist text. No command interpreter, shell invocation, or dynamic execution path is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts match ordinary words in the recovery documentation, such as recover, checkpoint, and previous session. No hashing, encryption, key handling, host discovery, or system probing is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are natural-language recovery instructions and placeholders. There is no cryptographic API usage or system reconnaissance command in SKILL.md.

content.value_statement: Long AI sessions can lose important task context after resets or context limits. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a structured memory-keeper recovery process.
actual_capabilities: Loads recent memory-keeper context items for a previous session.
Summarizes available context before work resumes.
Identifies current tasks, modified files, progress, next actions, and blockers.
Formats recovered state into a clear session summary.
Handles full, partial, and empty recovery outcomes.
limitations: Requires memory-keeper context to exist before recovery starts.
Cannot restore work that was never saved or checkpointed.
Does not verify file contents or git status by itself.
Needs user confirmation before continuing recovered work.

use_cases.title: Resume Development After Context Loss
use_cases.description: Restore the last task, modified files, blockers, and next action after a long coding session resets.

use_cases.title: Review Interrupted AI Work
use_cases.description: Summarize recent checkpoints so a user can decide whether to continue or restart.

use_cases.title: Recover Partial Session Notes
use_cases.description: Present available memory items and clearly mark missing information when recovery data is incomplete.

prompt_templates.title: Recover My Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Recover my previous session from memory-keeper and show the current task, progress, modified files, blockers, and next action.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner recovery after a session reset

prompt_templates.title: Find Recent Progress
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the recovery process to find recent progress items and summarize what changed before I continue.

prompt_templates.title: Rebuild Project State
prompt_templates.prompt: Recover the project state from recent checkpoints. Separate confirmed facts from missing or uncertain information.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Recovery Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the recovery checklist, report available checkpoints, identify blockers, and recommend the safest next action.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced recovery before resuming complex work

output_examples.input: Recover what I was working on before the session reset.
output_examples.output: Session recovered. Previous task: implement the checkout validation flow. Progress: validation rules were drafted, but tests remain. Files modified: checkout form and validation helper. Recommended next action: review the helper and add focused tests.

output_examples.input: Do we have enough context to continue?
output_examples.output: Partial recovery found. Available information includes the active task and two recent checkpoints. Missing information includes exact file changes and blocker status. Recommendation: inspect the repository before continuing.

output_examples.input: Start fresh if there is no memory.
output_examples.output: No previous context found. There are no checkpoints or progress items in memory-keeper. Please describe the task you want to start.

best_practices: Checkpoint important task state before clearing or ending a session.
Ask for confirmed facts and missing information before resuming work.
Review recovered files and blockers before making new changes.
anti_patterns: Do not assume unsaved work can be recovered.
Do not continue complex work without user confirmation.
Do not treat partial memory as complete project truth.

faq.question: What does this skill recover?
faq.answer: It recovers available memory-keeper context, including recent tasks, progress, modified files, blockers, and next actions.

faq.question: Can it recover deleted files?
faq.answer: No. It only summarizes stored context. It does not restore files from disk, git, or backups.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file is Markdown guidance and contains no executable shell commands.

faq.question: What happens if no context exists?
faq.answer: The skill reports that no previous context was found and asks the user to provide a new task.

faq.question: Can it handle incomplete memory?
faq.answer: Yes. It reports available information and clearly lists missing details before asking how to proceed.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
